subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Title clearly frames the research focus on F1 qualifying and race outcomes
Recognisable structure with introduction, data analysis and conclusion sections
Most material is relevant to the research question
weaknesses: Excessive restatement of textbook definitions inflates length without advancing analysis
Redundant citations and duplicated figures interrupt conciseness
Section headings and tables are sometimes misplaced or repeated, weakening flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments consistently place this work at band 2: it shows basic coherence and a recognisable sequence but lacks clear logical links and contains noticeable unnecessary elements. Organisation is adequate but not fully clear, and relevance is mostly maintained despite repetition. These features warrant a score of 2 out of 4.

strengths: Uses correct statistical terminology (e.g. Pearsonâs correlation, median cases) and includes effective scatter-plot visuals
Some demonstration of median and regression calculations enhances understanding
Overall explanations are understandable in most parts
weaknesses: Frequent notation errors and misrendered symbols reduce clarity
Inconsistent decimal and citation formatting interrupts consistency
Key steps are occasionally omitted, leaving the logical thread uneven
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication shows some appropriate language and representations (band 2) but suffers from regular inconsistencies in notation, formatting glitches and skipped working. These moderate shortcomings support a score of 2 out of 4.

strengths: Clear personal rationale linking the topic to the studentâs F1 interest
weaknesses: Limited evidence of independent thinkingâthe methods follow standard textbook procedures
Minimal creativity or novel adaptation of techniques
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Personal engagement displays a personal interest but lacks significant independent insight or creative initiative, aligning with band 1. A score of 1 out of 3 reflects this limited engagement.

strengths: Acknowledges limitations such as small sample size and correlation vs. causation
Suggests directions for further analysis (e.g. pit strategies, weather models)
weaknesses: Reflection is superficial and not integrated throughout the exploration
Critical analysis is basic, with no deep evaluation or quantitative critique
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reflective comments are present but remain surface-level and do not deeply analyse methodological implications or enhance conceptual understanding. This corresponds to band 1, meriting 1 out of 3.

strengths: Employs SL-level mathematics (means, medians, Pearson and Spearman coefficients, linear regression) directly addressing the research question
Regression equations and correlation values are clearly stated and interpreted
Demonstrates awareness of data cleaning and rank-based correlation nuances
weaknesses: Several computational slips and miswritten formulas undermine accuracy
Notation errors (e.g. incorrect mean formula) and inconsistent summation symbols appear
Methodological choices (Pearson vs Spearman) lack explicit justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is clearly relevant and at the expected course level (band 3) but marred by accuracy issues and notation mistakes. These factors justify a mid-band score of 3 out of 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The choice of topic is personally motivated, yet the mathematical path (basic correlation and regression) follows standard textbook procedures without novel elements.  The investigation does not extend beyond routine calculations, so evidence of independent thinking is limited.

The student demonstrates some understanding by describing what the coefficients signify and discussing external factors, yet explanations are mostly paraphrased textbook definitions and occasionally misuse terminology.  Knowledge is evident but not thorough or rigorous, aligning with band 2.

Several computational slips exist (incorrect mean formula, sign errors in Pearson calculation table, discrepancy in Spearman value formatting).  Scatter-plot regression equations appear plausible, so much of the mathematics is correct, but consistent accuracy is lacking.  Partial correctness matches band 2.

Most of the material is relevant to the research question, yet large portions simply restate textbook definitions (means, medians, Pearson, Spearman, regression) without advancing the investigation.  Redundant quotations and duplicated citations make the write-up longer than necessary.  Consequently the exploration is mostly relevant but contains noticeable unnecessary elements, matching band 2.

Mathematical terminology (e.g. âPearsonâs product-moment correlation coefficientâ) is used and symbols appear, but many are mis-rendered (\(\bar{x}\) shown as â(\bar{x})â, random overlines for integers, etc.).  Variables are not consistently defined, and the mean formula is written incorrectly.  This indicates some appropriate language/notation but with regular inaccuracies, consistent with band 2.

Explanations are understandable in most parts, but symbol misuse and formatting glitches reduce consistency; for instance, the same source is cited twice in the bibliography and the text alternates between â2022â and â2023â.  Mathematical working is occasionally skipped, making the narrative uneven.  Overall communication is mostly clear with some inconsistency.

The mathematics (means, medians, Pearson/Spearman coefficients, linear regression) is squarely at SL level and all directly address the research question, so relevance is clear.  However, the scope remains modest and does not move into more sophisticated territory such as hypothesis testing or multivariate analysis, fitting band 3 (course-level mathematics used).

The student employs tables, two scatter plots with regression lines, and briefly mentions linear equations; these give more than a minimal representation, yet figure titles are duplicated and one table is incomplete.  No residual plots, boxplots or additional visuals are provided.  Thus there is some appropriate use of representations but it is limited in range and quality, warranting band 2.

The report includes brief reflective comments in the conclusion (acknowledging limitations and external factors), but these are surface-level statements rather than sustained reflection woven through the analysis.  Hence depth of reflection is limited.

The work applies common statistical tools without any inventive adaptation (e.g., no creative visualisations, modelling variants, or novel data treatment).  Initiative is limited to selecting two races; therefore only some minimal creativity is evident.

The student follows a recognisable sequence (introduction â data â analysis â conclusion) and the argument generally progresses from description to calculation to short interpretation.  However, logical links are often weak: for example, the mean/median discussion is not connected back to correlation, and several explanatory paragraphs are repeated almost verbatim.  Abrupt jumps (e.g., formula derivations that do not use the symbols defined) break the chain of reasoning, so overall coherence is only moderate rather than clear and sustained.

Some basic critique appearsâfor example, noting that âcorrelation is not causationâ and that two races constitute a small sampleâbut the exploration does not analyse the impact of identified weaknesses or propose specific remedies.  This corresponds to basic, limited critical analysis.

Links between reflection and mathematical understanding are superficial; statements like âthis shows a strong relationshipâ restate results rather than deepening conceptual insight.  The connection to understanding is therefore limited.

There is an identifiable structure with titled sections and a bibliography, but headings such as âDistributionâ and âResultsâ are not always placed where the reader expects, and some tables/figures are omitted or duplicated.  Page fragments ("{3}---") and mis-aligned tables further weaken the structural clarity.  Hence organisation is adequate but clearly below the standard of a well-organised report.

Personal engagement is visible in the introductory rationale and casual references to the studentâs F1 interest, but this personal voice scarcely guides the method or interpretation.  The approach remains generic rather than being clearly driven by the studentâs own perspective, placing it in band 1.

annotations.comment: The title clearly states the research focus and context, providing a strong initial framing for the exploration.
The administrative details line is unnecessary to the mathematical exploration and could be removed to improve conciseness.
The introduction provides good background on F1 qualifying and racing, but repeats information; consider streamlining to maintain relevance.
The rationale articulates a clear personal interest in the topic, linking it to broader discussions about race outcomes.
The aim and plan outline multiple methods but lack justification for selecting Pearson vs Spearman; add reasoning to strengthen the methodological rationale.
The personal engagement section identifies interest in statistics but does not link this to deeper independent insight; consider reflecting on how your interest shaped method choices.
The raw tables show misrendered symbols (e.g. overlines on integers) which hinders readability; ensure consistent notation for all data entries.
The explanation of data cleaning (excluding non-finishers) is concise and shows awareness of dataset relevance.
Definitions of mean and median are quoted at length without need; paraphrase and integrate them to maintain flow and relevance.
The mean formula is incorrectly written as $$\frac{1}{x}=\frac{\Sigma x}{N}$$; it should be $$\bar x=\frac{\sum x_i}{N}$$ to be mathematically correct.
The detailed median formula block demonstrates correct understanding of cases for odd and even n.
The median calculation equation on page 6 is syntactically confusing; break into steps to show how terms combine rather than nested fractions.
Pearsonâs formula is presented but symbols for means and summations are mis-typed (e.g. \(x - x\) instead of \(x - \bar x\)). Revise to standard notation.
The description of calculating components for Pearsonâs r in Excel is clear, showing methodological transparency.
The tabulated calculations omit the headers for means \(\bar x\) and \(\bar y\), making it hard to follow; include these labels for clarity.
The computed Pearson values (r = 0.923 and 0.711) are interpreted correctly as strong positive correlations.
The description of external factors is superficial; deepen critical reflection by explaining how specific factors might quantitatively affect correlation values.
Spearmanâs formula and discussion of tied ranks demonstrate awareness of rank-based correlation nuances.
The Spearman result uses a comma for decimal (0,744) rather than a point; maintain consistent decimal notation throughout.
The rank tables exhibit misrendered symbols and inconsistent formatting (e.g. stray \(\sqrt{}\)); standardize labels and clean OCR artifacts.
The set of race-specific correlation results is clearly summarized, reinforcing comparison between years.
The introduction to linear regression explains the concept well, but omits a link back to how it addresses the research question.
The scatter plots in Figures 2 and 3 effectively show the positive trend and enhance understanding through visual representation.
Figure captions are duplicated and placed inconsistently; ensure each figure has a unique label and caption adjacent to it.
The regression equations $$y=0.617x+3.419$$ and $$y=0.905x+0.850$$ are clearly stated, demonstrating proper use of technology.
The conclusion restates results effectively but reflection on implications is brief; consider deeper critique of methodology and findings.
The discussion section aptly suggests further investigation of pit strategies and weather models, showing initiative for extended analysis.
Bibliography entries 7 and 8 are duplicates; remove redundancy and ensure each source is cited once.
Citation style is inconsistent (dates, access formats); adopt a single referencing style (e.g. APA) for all entries.
